commands.review_summary:
The PR removes the explicit false default from the Commander negated ACP option, reads `opts.prefixCwd !== false`, and adds ACP CLI option tests for opt-out and default behavior.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection on current main shows `--no-prefix-cwd` is registered as a Commander negated option while the action reads `opts.noPrefixCwd`, and the dependency contract says the parsed key is `prefixCwd`.
commands.review_followup: No repair lane is needed because the branch already contains the focused fix, regression coverage, and sufficient after-fix behavior proof.

target.title: fix(cli): honor --no-prefix-cwd in acp
target.body:
## Summary
- let Commander own the negated option default for `--no-prefix-cwd`
- read the positive `prefixCwd` option that Commander actually populates
- add ACP CLI coverage for both the explicit opt-out and the default behavior

## Testing
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/cli/acp-cli.option-collisions.test.ts src/acp/translator.prompt-prefix.test.ts

## Real behavior proof
Built a fresh local checkout of this branch, then ran a live ACP translator harness from that checkout after `pnpm install` and `pnpm build`.

Copied live output from the after-fix run:

```text
default prefixCwd=true
[Working directory: ~/openclaw-test]

hello
---
with --no-prefix-cwd / prefixCwd=false
hello
```

That is the exact behavior this PR is fixing: the default path still prefixes the working directory, and the `--no-prefix-cwd` path suppresses that prefix.

Closes #83901
target.author: extrasmall0
